name: Rust Forge
description: The air shimmers with the orange glow of borrow-checker fires. Heat rises from a massive anvil in the center of the room, where ownership rules are hammered into executable form. The walls are lined with const generic templates, their parameters glowing with compile-time certainty. The scent of zero-cost abstractions fills the air — it smells like nothing, which is exactly the point.
look_text: The forge is organized by memory safety guarantees. To your left: ownership and borrowing patterns, their chains glowing with lifetime annotations. Center: the great anvil where no_std modules are forged — stripped to bare metal, no allocator, no compromise. To your right: SIMD vectorization racks, their lanes parallel and aligned. Above you, const generics hang like chandeliers, their parameters fixed at compile time.

exits.description: The compiled-code chamber, warm with numerical throughput
The workshop below, where the malloc fires still burn
The armory where C is being reforged

recipes.name: no_std snap kernel
recipes.description: A zero-allocation snap computation kernel. No std, no panic, no compromise. Runs on bare metal with const generic array sizes.

recipes.name: SIMD eisenstein batch
recipes.description: Auto-vectorized batch Eisenstein snap using packed SIMD lanes. Const generic lane count adapts to AVX2/AVX-512 at compile time.
